title: Microsoft IIS Failed Request Tracing (FREB) Log Analysis
dataSources: `index=web` Failed Request Tracing XML converted to JSON or `ms:iis:freb` via custom parser
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (`Splunk_TA_windows`); Splunk Add-on for Microsoft IIS (Splunkbase 3185)
spl:
index=web sourcetype="iis:freb" OR sourcetype="ms:iis:freb"
| stats count by url, status_code, failure_reason
| sort - count
| head 30
description: FREB captures per-request pipeline events—including modules, rewrite rules, and authentication—when requests fail or exceed thresholds. Aggregating failure reasons ranks fixes faster than coarse HTTP status counts alone.
value: Shortens performance and reliability incidents on Windows-only stacks without full code deploys.
implementation: Enable FREB for 5xx and long-running requests; ship `%SystemDrive%\inetpub\logs\FailedReqLogFiles`. Add `props.conf` XML breaking.
visualization: Stacked bars for status/substatus, Perfmon timecharts, top client tables.
grandmaExplanation: We turn IIS failed request traces into ranked problems for developers.
